texto: Although people like Deborah Lipstadt, the Emory University professor who has written and lectured widely on Holocaust denial, have exhorted Jewish parents to just say no to intermarriage, much the way they expect their children not to take drugs, a large majority of parents (and more than a few rabbis) are unable to lay down opposition to intermarriage as a strict operating principle.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Ellen J. McClain: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a familia. O trecho “Although people like Deborah Lipstadt, the Emory University professor who has written and lecture…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, deborah-e-lipstadt, racism pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de casamento e racism.
